title: Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Homeless Families - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Homeless Families 9 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Domestic Violence Issues Homeless Families Homeless Veterans Homeless Youth Human Trafficking Issues Pregnant Individuals Sexual Assault Issues Veterans Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO COORDINATED ENTRY FOR FAMILIES MULTNOMAH COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Multnomah County Coordinated Entry for Families is a partnership of different agencies through Multnomah County’s Homeless Service Department (HSD) and consists of both the Supportive Housing & Family Shelter program. 211info completes eligibility and program enrollment via a Multnomah Services and Screening Tool (MSST) assessment. If families are eligible, partnering agencies within the community connect with families and offer services related to shelter/housing access. Meeting eligibility requirements does not guarantee access to services. Households are prioritized for assistance based on various factors, including availability of resources. This is not an immediate service and the wait time can be a few months to a year. It is encouraged that households continue seeking other resources and support. This program CANNOT assist financially with evictions to maintain current housing. It may be able to help with future housing if the household becomes homeless. Eligibility: Families with children in their custody under the age of 18 (or plans for reunification) - OR - a third trimester pregnancy (28 weeks/7 months) with connections to Multnomah County (i.e., was displaced, has kids in school, job, service providers in Multnomah County, etc.) AND meet one of the following criteria: -In emergency shelter, transitional housing, motel paid for by an agency or a place not meant for habitation (i.e. car, streets, tent, abandoned building, etc.) -Doubled up (with family/friends and not on the lease, unsafely doubled up/living situation is over safe capacity) and at risk of becoming literally homeless within 14 days -Housed, including self pay at a motel, and will become literally homeless within 14 days or has received a court ordered eviction notice OR has a self-identified EHV voucher set to expire in Fall of 2026 -In an institution (i.e. jail, hospital, substance use or mental health treatment facility, etc.) and at risk of exiting to homelessness within 14 days -Fleeing or attempting to flee domestic violence and lacks the resources or support networks to obtain other safe, permanent housing Hours: Added to callback List for Assessment: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Assessment: 7 days per week 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people to housing programs, including shelter information, other housing resources, tenant support, and non-housing resources. Does not provide emergency housing or rental assistance, and there may be a waitlist. 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Must be a current participant of OASIS drop-in center Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-4pm, Wednesday 8am-6pm, closed holidays Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF EUGENE EUGENE COMMUNITY COURT LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people to housing programs, including shelter information, other housing resources, tenant support, and non-housing resources. Does not provide emergency housing or rental assistance, and there may be a waitlist. 1102 Lincoln Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Individuals age 18 and older or families with minor children who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Wednesday 1:30pm-3:30pm Email (541) 682-5400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CLACKAMAS COUNTY COORDINATED HOUSING ACCESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a centralized access point for households experiencing homelessness or a housing crisis through a collaboration of multiple agencies. Eligible households are reviewed for services and added to the Housing By Name List for all qualifying programs. Assistance with rent or moving costs is limited, not guaranteed, and may depend on factors such as urban growth boundaries, eviction notices, court dates, and funding. Eligibility: Individuals and families who are homeless or at imminent risk of becoming homeless seeking prevention/diversion services to stay in their current homes Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Off-peak call times: Monday-Thursday 4pm-6pm Email (503) 655-8575 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD JACKSON COUNTY CONTINUUM OF CARE SCREENINGS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF This is an access point for being placed on Jackson County's Centralized Interest List for housing. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or families with children ages 18 and younger, who are experiencing homelessnesss Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people to housing programs, including shelter information, other housing resources, tenant support, and non-housing resources. Does not provide emergency housing or rental assistance, and there may be a waitlist. 4060 W Amazon Drive Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Families with minor children who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: 7 days per week 9am-5pm (541) 342-7728 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WHITE BIRD CLINIC LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people to housing programs, including shelter information, other housing resources, tenant support, and non-housing resources. Does not provide emergency housing or rental assistance, and there may be a waitlist. 323 E 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Individuals age 18 and older or families with minor children who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday/Friday 10am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 342-1295 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP ANYDOOR PLACE MCMINNVILLE DAY CENTER ANYDOOR PLACE COORDINATED ENTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides housing-related coordinated entry. Services may include assistance with past-due rent and rental deposit support, ongoing housing assistance, connection to help with utilities, assistance with apartment searching, and referrals to shelters and other local resources. Also provides case management. 327 SW Adams Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with minor children Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-noon (503) 687-5783 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP ANYDOOR PLACE COORDINATED ENTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides housing-related coordinated entry. Services may include assistance with past-due rent and rental deposit support, ongoing housing assistance, connection to help with utilities, assistance with apartment searching, and referrals to shelters and other local resources. Also provides case management. 1317 NE Dustin Court McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with minor children Hours: Phone and email: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 472-0712 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS CITY OF EUGENE LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS MASLOW PROJECT JACKSON COUNTY CONTINUUM OF CARE SCREENINGS ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS WHITE BIRD CLINIC LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP ANYDOOR PLACE COORDINATED ENTRY YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP ANYDOOR PLACE COORDINATED ENTRY
